content_markdown:
A container is filled with a mixture of helium and oxygen at the same temperature. The molar mass of helium is $4\text{ g mol}^{-1}$ and that of oxygen is $32\text{ g mol}^{-1}$.

What is the ratio $\frac{\text{average speed of helium molecules}}{\text{average speed of oxygen molecules}}$?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

vibe_explanation: Since both gases are at the same temperature, their average kinetic energies are equal: $\frac{1}{2}m_{He}v_{He}^2 = \frac{1}{2}m_{O}v_{O}^2$. This implies that the ratio of their speeds is inversely proportional to the square root of the ratio of their masses: $\frac{v_{He}}{v_{O}} = \sqrt{\frac{m_{O}}{m_{He}}}$. Substituting the molar masses, we get $\sqrt{\frac{32}{4}} = \sqrt{8}$.
